title: elatine paludosa subsp. macropoda
intro: Elatine paludosa subsp. macropoda, also known as marsh ellipter, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the family Elatinaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elatine paludosa subsp. macropoda include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
